context: Items where Gemini passes disagreed. Review and classify.

items.text: Dane shielded his eyes. His retinas prickled with heat. Not the gentle warmth of morning light, but the sting of something caustic, something that wanted to get inside.
items.gemini_reasoning: Describes Dane's subjective physical sensation and perception of the light.

items.text: His retinas prickled with heat.
items.gemini_reasoning: Specific tactile and visual description.

items.text: Milutin was staring directly at the risen sun. His eyes were wide open. He wasn't squinting.
items.gemini_reasoning: Visual description emphasizing Milutin's unusual reaction to the intense light.

items.text: His eyes were wide open. He wasn't squinting.
items.gemini_reasoning: Specific visual description of Milutin's eyes.

items.text: The sun. It's the wrong color.
items.gemini_reasoning: Dane's subjective observation and belief about the sun's appearance.

items.text: His face carried the blankness of a man who has just woken from surgery and doesn't yet know what has been removed.
items.gemini_reasoning: Describes Milutin's facial expression, indicating a subjective state of confusion or memory loss.

items.text: It's white. ... It used to be yellow. Yesterday it was yellow.
items.gemini_reasoning: A significant discovery made by Dane: the sun's color has changed from yellow to white.

items.text: A furrow appeared between his father's brows. For one moment, Dane saw something flicker behind the eyes???a hesitation, a fragment of memory trying to surface through murky water. Then the expression smoothed.
items.gemini_reasoning: Dane's observation of Milutin's brief internal struggle and subsequent return to a blank state.

items.text: The sun has always been white, Dane.
items.gemini_reasoning: Statement presented as fact by Milutin, which contradicts Dane's memory and is part of the narrative's altered reality/lore.

items.text: Why would it be yellow?
items.gemini_reasoning: A rhetorical question posed by Milutin, which, within the narrative's context of altered memory, highlights the absence of the 'yellow sun' concept and is intended to remain unanswered to preserve the mystery.

items.text: He didn't remember.
items.gemini_reasoning: A direct disclosure that Milutin has no memory of the sun being yellow.

items.text: Milutin showed no pain. The new light was his light now.
items.gemini_reasoning: Describes Milutin's lack of physical discomfort and his internal acceptance/assimilation of the changed light.

items.text: The old light had been written over, and his mind had smoothed itself over so quickly that no seam remained???no ridge, no roughness, nothing to suggest the cut had ever been made. The rewriting hadn't stopped at telegraph wires. It had reached into his father's eyes and adjusted the lens. It had opened the filing cabinet of his memory and removed every folder labeled 'yellow.' It had performed surgery on a living man without anesthesia, without incision, without leaving any scar except the absence of what used to be there.
items.gemini_reasoning: Detailed description of a series-invented mechanism (the 'rewriting') affecting memory and perception.

items.text: his mind had smoothed itself over so quickly that no seam remained???no ridge, no roughness, nothing to suggest the cut had ever been made.
items.gemini_reasoning: Metaphorical description of how Milutin's mind adapted to the new reality.

items.text: It had opened the filing cabinet of his memory and removed every folder labeled 'yellow.'
items.gemini_reasoning: Figurative language used to describe the erasure of specific memories.

items.text: It had performed surgery on a living man without anesthesia, without incision, without leaving any scar except the absence of what used to be there.
items.gemini_reasoning: Metaphorical description of the memory alteration process, emphasizing its invasiveness and subtlety.

items.text: The filter was gone. And Milutin didn't know he'd ever had one.
items.gemini_reasoning: A key disclosure: a 'filter' related to perception is gone, and Milutin is unaware of its prior existence, signifying a deep alteration of reality and memory.

items.text: Dane had retreated to the cellar beneath his father's church.
items.gemini_reasoning: Describes Dane's action motivated by the altered environment.

items.text: The limestone walls were three feet thick. The oak door was heavy enough to muffle the bells on Easter Sunday.
items.gemini_reasoning: Specific descriptive language appealing to touch, sight, and sound, detailing the cellar's protective qualities.

items.text: the light found him anyway. A single beam pierced through a crack in the foundation???a fissure no wider than a finger, running through the mortar where the stones met. The beam hit the dirt floor like a laser. Not a shaft of dusty morning light. A cylinder. Solid. The edges sharp enough to cut.
items.gemini_reasoning: Description of the light's unusual physical properties and penetrative power, which is part of the series' invented canon.

items.text: fissure no wider than a finger
items.gemini_reasoning: Specific visual description of the crack.

items.text: Where the beam touched the soil, the color drained instantly. Brown dirt turned bone-white in a perfect circle, as if something had reached down and siphoned the pigment away.
items.gemini_reasoning: Description of the light's supernatural effect on physical objects, draining color, which is part of the series' invented canon.

items.text: as if something had reached down and siphoned the pigment away.
items.gemini_reasoning: Figurative language describing the color-draining effect of the light.

items.text: Brown dirt turned bone-white in a perfect circle
items.gemini_reasoning: Specific visual description of the soil's change.

items.text: Dane pressed himself against the far wall. The stone was cool against his back. But even here, in the deepest shadows, the weight found him. Not pressure from outside. Pressure from above. The sensation of a massive, patient attention pressing down through three feet of limestone, through oak beams, through the cool dark air of the cellar. A hand on the crown of his skull, holding him still while something decided what to do with him.
items.gemini_reasoning: Dane's subjective physical and psychological experience of an unseen force.

items.text: A massive, patient attention pressing down... while something decided what to do with him.
items.gemini_reasoning: The source and intent of this 'attention' are deliberately left unknown, inviting speculation.

items.text: He closed his eyes. The light didn't go away.
items.gemini_reasoning: Dane's subjective experience of the light's persistence despite closing his eyes.

items.text: Eyelids are thin. Translucent membranes designed to filter, not to block. Against the red darkness of his own blood, Dane could see the branching map of his retinal vessels???every vein, every capillary, illuminated from outside like a stained glass window lit by a fire he couldn't escape.
items.gemini_reasoning: Explanation of why the light penetrates eyelids, revealing a specific property of the light within the series' canon.

items.text: Against the red darkness of his own blood, Dane could see the branching map of his retinal vessels???every vein, every capillary
items.gemini_reasoning: Specific visual description of Dane's internal vision.

items.text: illuminated from outside like a stained glass window lit by a fire he couldn't escape.
items.gemini_reasoning: Figurative language describing Dane's internal vision of his retinal vessels under the light.

items.text: And behind the red, something else. The grid. Faint grey lines crossing his vision. Triangles overlaying everything he looked at, tracking when he turned his head, following when he shifted his gaze. He opened his eyes. The grid remained. It wasn't in the sky anymore. It was in his optic nerve.
items.gemini_reasoning: Major discovery by Dane: a 'grid' is appearing in his internal vision, and it has become part of his optic nerve.

items.text: Faint grey lines crossing his vision. Triangles overlaying everything he looked at
items.gemini_reasoning: Specific visual description of the 'grid'.

items.text: It wasn't in the sky anymore. It was in his optic nerve.
items.gemini_reasoning: This describes a change in the nature or location of the 'grid', establishing it as an internal phenomenon within the series' canon.

items.text: He watched the white circle on the floor pulse???brighter, dimmer, brighter???as if something was modulating the intensity.
items.gemini_reasoning: Dane's subjective observation of the light's pulsating behavior.

items.text: pulse???brighter, dimmer, brighter
items.gemini_reasoning: Specific visual description of the light's intensity change.

items.text: as if something was modulating the intensity.
items.gemini_reasoning: The source of the modulation is left unknown, contributing to the mystery.

items.text: His hands pressed into the cold dirt. His knees left tracks in the dust. The closer he got to the beam, the heavier the air became. It resisted him. Each inch forward required effort???not because he was tired, but because the atmosphere itself had opinions about where he should go.
items.gemini_reasoning: Dane's physical actions and subjective experience of the environment's resistance.

items.text: His hands pressed into the cold dirt. ... the heavier the air became.

items.text: the atmosphere itself had opinions about where he should go.

items.text: It pushed back. It wanted him to stay in the shadows.
items.gemini_reasoning: Description of the light/atmosphere exhibiting agency, which is part of the series' invented canon.

items.text: The light/atmosphere actively resists and tries to prevent Dane from approaching it.
shadow on one side, blinding white on the other, with no gradient between.
items.gemini_reasoning: Specific visual description of the stark boundary of the light.

items.text: The boundary was absolute???shadow on one side, blinding white on the other, with no gradient between.
items.gemini_reasoning: Describes a specific, unnatural property of the light's boundary, establishing it as part of the series' canon.

items.text: Static crackled across his knuckles. The fine hairs rose and stayed rigid, reaching toward the beam

items.text: The sensation was not heat. It was thickness. His hand entered the beam as if passing through warm syrup. The light had viscosity. It clung to his skin, resisted his movement, made every millimeter of progress feel like swimming through honey.
